rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.reason: Source-domain page on Saraiki dohra poetry. Title 'Saraiki Dohra Poetry' and snippet 'Saraiki dohra two-line couplet poetic form' contain the words 'saraiki', 'dohra', and 'couplet' but not the full content phrase 'saraiki dohra couplet' as a contiguous substring. No LLM relevance. Not prior art for the LLM mechanism.

Anchor-token decoding intervenes on a single anchor for hallucination control. DOHRA-DECODE proposes N parallel branches conditioned on a shared anchor with majority-vote reconciliation - structurally different. No content-word substring matches. Functional overlap is partial (shared anchor-token concept) but does not implement N-branch parallel redundancy or voting.
Semantic anchors in in-context learning; discusses reliance on label-anchor priors. No parallel-decoder mechanism, no N-branch redundancy, no voting. No content-word substring matches.

PASTA does parallel decoding of semantically-INDEPENDENT chunks - explicitly contrasted in the novelty claim. DOHRA-DECODE branches are deliberately REDUNDANT around an anchor with voting, not independent. No content-word substring matches. Mechanism is the inverse (independence vs redundancy) so functional equivalence is below threshold.

PPD uses guess-and-verify parallel prompt decoding with special tokens. Not N-branch redundant generation around a shared anchor with majority vote. Verification is single-path, not vote-based reconciliation across redundant branches. No content-word substring matches.
Parallel token prediction is about predicting multiple future tokens in parallel, not N redundant branches conditioned on a shared anchor with voting. No content-word substring matches.
Multi-token prediction with a draft head, similar to Medusa/speculative-decoding family. No anchor-conditioned N-branch redundant generation with majority vote. No content-word substring matches.
Learn2PD uses a lightweight filter to predict whether a token matches final output for parallel decoding. Not anchor-grounded redundant N-branch generation. No content-word substring matches.

fail_reason: No result reaches kw>=2, sem>=0.7, or func>=0.7. PASTA (rank 4) is the closest LLM analog but is explicitly the contrast case (independent chunks vs DOHRA's redundant branches around a shared anchor with majority-vote reconciliation); ICLR 2025 anchor-token decoding (rank 2) manipulates a single anchor for hallucination control rather than running N parallel branches with vote-based reconciliation. The specific combination of (1) shared-anchor conditioning, (2) N parallel branches generating semantically-redundant chunks, and (3) majority-vote/anchor-grounded reconciliation does not appear in the surfaced 2024-2026 literature.
